topic: Mag-gravity coupling constant kappa = 1.67 nT/uGal - c5 post-EXP-192 review
change_summary: EXP-192 integrated by decider commit 1651415 (2026-04-21 06:31 UTC). detail_evidence grew 5190 -> 8330 chars (+60%). New content: (a) dome live-site 'cannot discriminate' quote with Chebychev/Level-3 pipeline paraphrase; (b) new paragraph on WIN-011 concession to 'CONFIRMED (Mechanism Contested)' + thermal-tilt loophole + Unnikrishnan et al. 2001 DOI; (c) 'Even granting the dome its preferred numerical value for the sake of argument' charitability hedge; (d) Yang & Wang 2002 re-attribution with co-authorship framing; (e) unverified 'electromagnetic, not gravitational' quote removed. Fresh adversarial pass on the new text.
our_argument_summary: WIN-012 now argues: (1) the dome live-site WIN-013 pipeline rescue ('Chebychev low-pass attenuates contact-phase transients') is itself fatal to kappa because the same pipeline would filter the Mohe -6.5 uGal; (2) the dome WIN-011 concession to 'thermal-tilt loophole' makes the kappa denominator instrument artifact, not gravity; (3) the Yang & Wang 2002 timing analysis shows valleys at C1/C4 contacts, opposite gravitational-shielding expectation; (4) circular derivation from two different eclipses at two different sites; (5) hardcoded monitor.py identity check; (6) Exhibit A GRACE forensics unchanged. Verdict 'Self-Contradicted' retained and strengthened. However, on fresh reading, the new text has moved the critique in a direction the dome has already moved past - see holes_found.

dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: TWO new live-site escape clauses NOT ENGAGED by our text. WIN-013: "The 1999 eclipse pre-dated the contact-phase protocol - measurements were not optimized for C1/C4 timing correlation." WIN-014: "The 2009 campaign searched for totality-peak or shielding-style effects, not the contact-phase kappa-scaled pulse the ECM now predicts." These retconn both SG nulls as non-tests of the current prediction. Separately, WIN-011 now asserts a NEW mechanism: "The dielectric boundary transition model predicts maximum effect at C1/C4 specifically - the onset and termination of firmament-plate shading drives the unidirectional induction pulse." This reframes C1/C4 contact-phase gravity signals as a POSITIVE prediction of the dome (not as an artifact to be explained away).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The monitor.py code_analysis remains accurate. Prose-level mischaracterizations: (a) review cites "E-PRED-C" as a dome prediction label - no such label exists on the live site (verified 2026-04-21); (b) review states the dome "predicts 0.0 uGal during eclipses" - dome actually predicts -10 to -13 uGal pulse at C1/C4 contacts (PRED-TIER-2). See holes_found.

The new PRED-TIER-2 forward prediction is a pre-registered claim about the 2026-08-12 eclipse; it does not alter the monitor.py validation tag for the historical WIN.

code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c4. BOU 2017 and Mohe 1997 are pre-existing literature values. The kappa = 1.67 ratio was computed after both measurements existed; the derivation path from dome geometry to 1.67 does not exist.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c4. No dome-geometry derivation of 1.67 exists. The value is a ratio of two adopted measurements from different events.

holes_found.description: Fabricated citation: "E-PRED-C" prediction label does not exist on the dome live site. Our text asserts "its own eclipse prediction model (E-PRED-C) predicts 0.0 uGal during eclipses" as the closing beat of the detail_evidence. WebFetch of both https://john09289.github.io/predictions/ and https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html on 2026-04-21 returns NO prediction labeled "E-PRED-C" and NO prediction of "0.0 uGal during eclipses". The dome's actual eclipse gravity prediction is PRED-TIER-2: "10-13 uGal downward pulse at C1/C4 contact phases" (verified live). "E-PRED-C" is an INTERNAL label used in our own monitor/analyst/gravity-dependency-map.json and EXP-192 notes - not a dome-facing label. A dome defender who searches for E-PRED-C on the source site will find nothing and will (correctly) say "you cite a prediction label that does not exist." This is the same class of citation-integrity error c4 flagged for the "Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational" quote - we fixed that one and silently introduced a new one. SEVERITY: critical because the line is rhetorical payoff (it closes the last paragraph) and because the number it cites (0.0 uGal) is factually incompatible with the dome's live PRED-TIER-2 (-10 to -13 uGal).
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the closing clause "and its own eclipse prediction model (E-PRED-C) predicts 0.0 uGal during eclipses" with verified live-site language. Suggested: "and the dome's own current eclipse gravity prediction (PRED-TIER-2, predictions.html, accessed 2026-04-21) calls for a -10 to -13 uGal downward pulse at C1/C4 contact phases - a value incompatible with BOTH the 1997 Mohe -6.5 uGal datum (50-100% discrepancy, same site, same instrument class) AND the 1999/2009 SG nulls (which if PRED-TIER-2 is right must be instrument failures, which the dome explains by its own Level-3 pipeline filtering out exactly the signal it predicts)." This turns the live prediction into a stronger lever than the phantom label.

holes_found.description: Review is arguing against a dome position the author has already moved past. Our text frames Yang & Wang 2002's C1/C4 valleys as "exactly the contact-phase timing pattern a thermal-tilt artifact would produce, and the opposite of what gravitational shielding would predict (peak at maximum lunar coverage)." But the dome's CURRENT live-site WIN-011 explicitly asserts: "The dielectric boundary transition model predicts maximum effect at C1/C4 specifically - the onset and termination of firmament-plate shading drives the unidirectional induction pulse" (verified 2026-04-21). The dome has abandoned the "maximum-shielding-at-maximum-coverage" prediction our critique is refuting; the dome now claims its model PREDICTS C1/C4 signals. A dome defender will read our Yang-Wang critique and say: "Valleys at C1/C4 are exactly what the ECM dielectric model predicts. You're refuting gravitational shielding, which is the mechanism we abandoned. Our CURRENT mechanism (dielectric boundary transition) is consistent with Yang & Wang 2002." This does NOT save kappa (the dome now has to explain why SG pipelines filter out the signal it claims to predict, and why the Mohe -6.5 uGal is half the PRED-TIER-2 magnitude), but it means our attack lands on an empty position.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a short paragraph engaging the dielectric boundary transition model. Suggested text: "The dome has recently reframed C1/C4 gravity signals as a POSITIVE prediction of its model - the live WIN-011 cites a 'dielectric boundary transition model' in which 'the onset and termination of firmament-plate shading drives the unidirectional induction pulse' at first and last contact. This concedes that the Mohe -6.5 uGal valleys ARE what the dome expects - but creates three new problems: (i) the live PRED-TIER-2 prediction of -10 to -13 uGal at C1/C4 is 50-100% larger in magnitude than the -6.5 uGal measurement from which kappa was originally derived; (ii) the dome's SG-null rescue ('Chebychev low-pass attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release') says the pipeline filters out the very signal the dielectric model predicts, making the forward prediction untestable by any Level-3 SG observation; (iii) kappa = 1.67 was derived under the 2000 SHIELDING interpretation the dome has now abandoned - re-using the same numerical value after retreating to a different mechanism is parameter adoption, not prediction." This redirects our attack onto the dome's current position.

holes_found.description: WIN-013/014 live-site escape clauses not engaged. The dome's live WIN-013 now adds: "The 1999 eclipse pre-dated the contact-phase protocol - measurements were not optimized for C1/C4 timing correlation." WIN-014 adds: "The 2009 campaign searched for totality-peak or shielding-style effects, not the contact-phase kappa-scaled pulse the ECM now predicts." (Both verified 2026-04-21.) These retcon the SG nulls as non-tests of the current prediction. Our text treats WIN-013/014 as straightforward null-result confirmations of the kappa self-contradiction. The dome's live framing is softer and is not rebutted. A dome defender will say: "We never claimed SGs at totality would see a shielding signal; our dielectric prediction is at C1/C4 and our pipeline filters contact-phase transients. The 1999 and 2009 campaigns weren't looking for C1/C4 pulses. Your 'denominator vanishes' argument is applying our old shielding framing to observations that weren't set up to test our new framing." The rebuttal is scientifically weak (SG data is continuous, so C1/C4 data exists in both 1999 and 2009 records; and the pipeline-filters-own-prediction structure is self-defeating), but the escape clauses should be named and defeated, not ignored.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a sentence to the "Internal contradiction with WIN-013/014" paragraph: "The dome's live WIN-013 now adds the escape clause 'measurements were not optimized for C1/C4 timing correlation' and WIN-014 adds 'the 2009 campaign searched for totality-peak or shielding-style effects, not the contact-phase kappa-scaled pulse the ECM now predicts.' Both retcon the SG nulls as non-tests of the current prediction. Both are scientifically weak: SG data from 1999 (Membach) and 2009 (Chinese network) is continuously sampled across the full eclipse including C1/C4, and a -10 to -13 uGal pulse over a few minutes at contact would exceed the SG noise floor. The retconn is indistinguishable from an unfalsifiable retreat: any prior null becomes 'the wrong test' when the prediction moves."

holes_found.description: Bracketed quote modification "[attenuation of]" transforms the dome's verbatim verb "attenuates" into a gerund. The live-site exact text is: "Chebychev low-pass attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release" (verified 2026-04-21). Our review renders this as: "Chebychev low-pass [attenuation of] contact-phase transients before Level-3 release." The square brackets are the scholarly convention for editorial insertion/substitution; here they re-form the main verb. A careful reader will see "attenuates" on the dome site and "[attenuation of]" in our review and conclude we have altered the quote to fit our sentence structure. The transformation is grammatical, not semantic, but quote-integrity is a tone we hold the dome to.
holes_found.recommendation: Either quote verbatim - reword the surrounding sentence so "Chebychev low-pass attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release" drops in unchanged - or paraphrase without quote marks. Do not use brackets to silently re-form the verb. Suggested: "because the ECM pipeline (in the dome's words) 'attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release'."

holes_found.description: "Preliminary report" characterization of Wang et al. (2000) is tendentious. Our text reads "revising the earlier Wang et al. 2000 preliminary report." Wang et al. (2000) was published as Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101(R) - a peer-reviewed Rapid Communication in a top-tier physics journal, not a preliminary or pre-print report. "Preliminary" frames the paper as incomplete, which is our rhetorical position but not the paper's self-description. A physicist reviewing our citation will note the framing and may conclude we are loading the dice. The 2002 paper is correctly described as a "reanalysis" or "subsequent paper"; "preliminary" overstates.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "preliminary report" with "rapid communication" (the paper's actual Phys. Rev. D category), or "earlier report" (neutral), or drop the descriptor: "... revising the earlier Wang et al. 2000 report (Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101(R))." Neutral framing preserves our argument without inviting a citation-rhetoric objection.

holes_found.description: "Retreating to a 'thermal-tilt loophole' for non-superconducting instruments as the only surviving mechanism" overclaims the dome's concession. The live WIN-011 (verified 2026-04-21) characterizes thermal tilt as one possibility: "Yang 2002 attempted tilt experiment but did not fully rule this out" - open, not accepted. The dome's CURRENT primary mechanism is the dielectric boundary transition model (explicit live-site text), with thermal tilt as an acknowledged alternative that has not been ruled out. Saying thermal tilt is "the only surviving mechanism" claims the dome has conceded more than it has - the dome's live text still proposes a non-thermal-tilt mechanism (dielectric boundary transition) and merely admits thermal tilt is a competing possibility. A dome defender will fetch WIN-011 and say: "We have not retreated to thermal tilt; we offer a dielectric mechanism and acknowledge thermal tilt as a known competing hypothesis. The review misreads our epistemic position."
holes_found.recommendation: Reword: instead of "retreating to a 'thermal-tilt loophole' for non-superconducting instruments as the only surviving mechanism," use "acknowledging that Unnikrishnan et al. (2001) rule out gravitational shielding and that Yang (2002)'s tilt experiment 'did not fully rule out' thermal tilt as a mechanism for the Mohe -6.5 uGal signal." Then continue: "Whether the dome's proposed dielectric-boundary-transition mechanism or Yang's thermal-tilt hypothesis is correct, both share a feature fatal to kappa: the -6.5 uGal datum is now interpreted as something other than the gravitational signal of lunar mass shielding - so a coupling constant between magnetism and gravitational shielding can no longer be 'confirmed' by it." This engages the dome's actual position and still lands the punch.

holes_found.description: Numerical consistency check between Mohe historical datum and dome's current forward prediction is missing. The dome now predicts PRED-TIER-2 = -10 to -13 uGal downward pulse at C1/C4 contact phases. The historical Mohe -6.5 uGal is the value from which kappa = 1.67 was derived. If the forward prediction is -10 to -13 uGal, the dome's CURRENT expectation is 54-100% larger in magnitude than the Mohe datum that supplied its own kappa. Same latitude class (both mid-latitude priority stations), same instrument class (ground-based spring gravimeters or SGs). If the forward prediction is correct, kappa = 1.67 times the BOU-class magnetic anomaly would produce ~10 uGal, not 6.5 uGal - meaning the kappa derivation is inconsistent with the dome's own forward math. This is a novel self-contradiction the review can lever; our current text does not mention PRED-TIER-2 at all.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a short computational paragraph: "The dome's current PRED-TIER-2 forward prediction (-10 to -13 uGal at C1/C4, predictions.html accessed 2026-04-21) is itself inconsistent with the -6.5 uGal Mohe datum that defines kappa. If kappa = 1.67 nT/uGal and the predicted delta B at priority stations is -17 to -21 nT (PRED-TIER-1), then delta g = delta B / kappa yields -10.2 to -12.6 uGal - consistent with PRED-TIER-2 but incompatible with the Mohe -6.5 uGal that anchors kappa itself. The dome's forward prediction uses a kappa whose own 'observed' value disagrees with the forward prediction by ~60% at the same coupling ratio. This is a novel self-contradiction: kappa cannot simultaneously yield the dome's historical -6.5 uGal 'confirmation' (WIN-012) and the dome's -10 to -13 uGal forward prediction (PRED-TIER-2)."

holes_found.description: TLDR currency check. tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict are unchanged from pre-EXP-192. tldr_verdict reads: "The dome claims both a 6.5 uGal detection (WIN-012) and 0.0 uGal null results (WIN-013/014) as simultaneous confirmations. If the signal is zero, the coupling constant is undefined - you can't have it both ways." This remains substantively accurate - the dome still lists WIN-013/014 as CONFIRMED with 0.0 uGal readings, and kappa is still undefined if the denominator vanishes - but the TLDR is now slightly behind the main text's more nuanced "cannot discriminate / thermal-tilt" framing. Not misleading; imprecise relative to the expanded content.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional minor update: "The dome simultaneously claims a 6.5 uGal eclipse gravity detection (WIN-012) and 0.0 uGal SG nulls (WIN-013/014) as confirmations, while reframing both as non-discriminating. Either way kappa's denominator is either undefined (if nulls stand) or not a gravity measurement (if the dome's current 'thermal tilt / dielectric' reinterpretation stands) - you can't have it all three ways." Low priority.

stronger_arguments.description: Engage with PRED-TIER-2 as the dome's own forward-predictive self-refutation. The dome predicts -10 to -13 uGal at C1/C4 contact phases in the 2026 eclipse - a value internally inconsistent with the -6.5 uGal Mohe datum from which kappa was derived. This turns the dome's forward prediction into a lever against WIN-012 without requiring the reader to accept our framing.

Engage with the dielectric boundary transition model explicitly. The dome now claims its model PREDICTS C1/C4 signals. Our Yang-Wang timing critique should be repositioned: the dome no longer claims peak-at-totality shielding, so the 'opposite of what shielding would predict' framing is fighting an empty position. The new critique: a model that predicts signal AND a pipeline that filters the same signal is structurally unfalsifiable.
sources: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html WIN-011 (fetched 2026-04-21) - 'The dielectric boundary transition model predicts maximum effect at C1/C4 specifically'

stronger_arguments.description: Credit the dome's live-site WIN-013/014 'cannot discriminate between the models' reframing as a concession. The dome admits the SG data does not distinguish its model from the globe model - that is itself a substantial walking-back of WIN-013/014 'CONFIRMED' status. Credit the walk-back, then note that a non-discriminating test is not a 'confirmed prediction' in the standard scientific sense, even though it appears in the dome's 'Confirmed Wins' column.
sources: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html WIN-013 (fetched 2026-04-21) - 'This result cannot discriminate between the models.'

kernel_of_truth.description: The EXP-192 rewrite is a substantive improvement over the c3/c4 text. It removes a verifiable-quote error, re-attributes Yang & Wang correctly, engages with the WIN-011 concession, and adds 'even granting' charitability. On every c4-flagged issue the new text is better. The dome, in parallel, has substantially improved WIN-011 - acknowledging Unnikrishnan 2001 rules out shielding, labeling the mechanism 'contested', and offering a falsifiable forward prediction (PRED-TIER-2) at a specific time (2026-08-12) at specific stations. Both sides have moved toward honest epistemic positions in the past month.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The verdict is unchanged ('Self-Contradicted') because: (i) kappa is still derived from the Mohe -6.5 uGal under a mechanism (shielding) the dome has now abandoned, so kappa is a fossilized parameter of a discarded interpretation; (ii) kappa's own forward prediction (PRED-TIER-2: -10 to -13 uGal) is numerically incompatible with the -6.5 uGal datum that anchors kappa; (iii) the dome's pipeline rescue for SG nulls says it filters out exactly the signal the dielectric model predicts - a self-sabotaging detector. The core self-contradiction is strengthened, not weakened, by the dome's honest updates.

deeper_analysis: Cycle 5 is the first fresh adversarial pass after EXP-192 full-rewrite integration. The rewrite landed every c4 recommendation substantively, so c4's holes are closed - credit to analyst and decider. But the rewrite hits a new problem: it engages the dome's position as of ~2026-04-07 to 2026-04-20 and has not tracked the dome's further updates in that window. The dome has (a) articulated a 'dielectric boundary transition model' predicting C1/C4 signals as a POSITIVE expectation (WIN-011 live), (b) added retconn escape clauses to WIN-013/014 ('not optimized for C1/C4 timing correlation', 'searched for totality-peak or shielding-style effects'), and (c) published specific forward predictions PRED-TIER-1/2/3 that our review does not cite at all. The result: our review is attacking a position the dome has moved past on the timing/mechanism question, and has introduced a new fabricated-label error (E-PRED-C, which does not exist on the dome site). The core verdict Self-Contradicted is unchanged and actually strengthened (see kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim), but the surface-level citation attack profile has regressed from c4: instead of fixing two citation errors in c4 we now have one new one plus two stale-framing issues, plus we are missing engagement with the dome's current forward predictions. Advocate-mode defense rates 3/5 for this cycle (up from 2/5 after c4, down from where c4's proposed fixes should have landed). The fixes are mechanical and a single coordinated patch (drop the E-PRED-C line, add PRED-TIER-2 engagement, add dielectric-boundary-transition paragraph, address WIN-013/014 retconn, reword 'preliminary report' and '[attenuation of]', reword 'only surviving mechanism') can produce a c6 text that is both citation-safe and engaged with the current dome position.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A capable dome defender reading the current WIN-012 detail_evidence would say: (1) 'You cite E-PRED-C as a prediction of mine. Search our site - no such label exists. Our actual eclipse gravity prediction is PRED-TIER-2: -10 to -13 uGal pulse at C1/C4 contacts. Please cite the real prediction.' (2) 'You write that Yang & Wang's C1/C4 valleys are the opposite of what gravitational shielding would predict. Correct - and we agree. We abandoned shielding. Our current mechanism is the dielectric boundary transition, which PREDICTS C1/C4 valleys. Your criticism confirms our prediction.' (3) 'You call Wang et al. 2000 a preliminary report. It is a Phys. Rev. D Rapid Communication, peer-reviewed. Please correct.' (4) 'You say WIN-013/014 are simultaneously claimed as confirmations. Read our live site: This result cannot discriminate between the models. We conceded the non-discriminating nature of the SG nulls.' (5) 'You characterize my position as only surviving mechanism = thermal tilt. Read WIN-011: I propose a dielectric boundary transition mechanism and acknowledge thermal tilt is another open possibility. You misrepresent my epistemic position.' All five rebuttals are factually correct as of 2026-04-21. The CORE self-contradiction (kappa is a historical artifact, pipeline filters predicted signal, forward magnitude mismatches historical datum) SURVIVES all five rebuttals, but the surface-level presentation looks like a review that hasn't tracked the dome for two weeks.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Single coordinated patch: (a) drop the E-PRED-C line entirely; replace with PRED-TIER-2 engagement per holes_found[0] recommendation; (b) add a dielectric-boundary-transition paragraph per holes_found[1] recommendation; (c) add WIN-013/014 retconn rebuttal per holes_found[2] recommendation; (d) fix '[attenuation of]' quote per holes_found[3]; (e) replace 'preliminary report' with 'rapid communication' or drop descriptor per holes_found[4]; (f) reword 'only surviving mechanism' per holes_found[5]; (g) add kappa-historical-magnitude-vs-forward-prediction paragraph per holes_found[6]. After the patch, the detail_evidence will be grounded in the dome's 2026-04-21 live position, will engage PRED-TIER-1/2/3 directly, and will turn the dome's forward predictions into ammunition. Verdict remains Self-Contradicted - strengthened by the PRED-TIER-2 vs Mohe datum numerical inconsistency.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-012's kappa = 1.67 implies delta g = delta B / kappa; for PRED-TIER-1 delta B = -17 to -21 nT, this gives delta g = -10.2 to -12.6 uGal, matching PRED-TIER-2. But kappa itself was computed from the Mohe -6.5 uGal - so PRED-TIER-2's magnitude is ~60% larger than the 'observation' that anchors kappa. This is a novel inconsistency introduced by the dome's own forward predictions, not yet engaged by our review. Recommend: engage PRED-TIER-1/2/3 in WIN-012 detail_evidence, and flag the parallel consistency issue in WIN-010/025 (which also reference kappa-class coupling).
cross_win_consistency.notes: No inconsistency introduced by EXP-192 between WIN-011 and WIN-012 - both now credit Yang & Wang 2002, both reference Unnikrishnan 2001 (via the live site), both use live-site language. Good cross-WIN discipline. The remaining cross-WIN issue is an inherited one: WIN-025 (Exhibit A 9-station storm data) relies on the same kappa = 1.67; if kappa is a historical artifact of a discarded mechanism, WIN-025's 'same coupling ratio' claim inherits the problem. Not in scope of this review; file as a companion issue if not already.
